paper_title: The hat guessing number of graphs
summary: The exact value of $HG(K_{n,n})$ remains unknown. The source paper established $\Omega(n^{1/2-o(1)}) = HG(K_{n,n}) \leq n+1$, with a substantial gap between the bounds. A 2021 follow-up by Alon and Chizewer (arXiv:2107.05995) studies the linear hat guessing number of complete multipartite graphs and restates the question as open, noting a positive answer seems plausible. No subsequent paper resolving the exact value was found in the indexed literature.

since_posted.title: On the Hat Guessing Number of Graphs
since_posted.claim: Considers the linear hat guessing number of complete multipartite graphs and restates the question of the exact value of $HG(K_{n,n})$ as open, noting a positive answer seems plausible without proving it.

internal_refs_verified.note: WebFetch of the abstract confirms the paper addresses complete multipartite graphs and the linear hat guessing number; full-text details on K_{n,n} bounds were not accessible, consistent with the internal reference description that the question is restated but not resolved.
This paper concerns list coloring choosability of bipartite graphs (attributed to Alon and Krivelevich), not hat guessing numbers; it appears to be a false positive match unrelated to HG(K_{n,n}).

search_queries: hat guessing number complete bipartite graph K_n,n exact value
hat guessing number graphs arxiv 1812.09752 follow-up
notes: The gap between the lower bound $\Omega(n^{1/2-o(1)})$ and the upper bound $n+1$ remains unresolved. The internal reference 2409.01513 (Bradshaw, Mohar, Stacho on choosability) appears to be a corpus match on bipartite graphs generally, not on hat guessing numbers specifically, and was not verified as relevant.
